title: Surface modified coal compositions
abstract: The present invention relates to activated coal compositions. The compositions of the invention could find use, for example, to mitigate ammonia emissions and reduce odours from animal (or intensive livestock) wastes by adsorbing ammonia and/or ammonium ions. The present invention also relates to methods of manufacturing surface modified activated coal compositions, and methods of using the same.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A method of activating a coal composition, comprising: (a) providing finely divided coal; and (b) heating the finely divided coal, under aerobic conditions, to provide the activated coal composition, wherein the aerobic conditions at step (b) comprise subjecting the finely divided coal to a stream of air, wherein the stream of air has a flow rate of between about 20 mL/min and 60 mL/min per 10 g sample of finely divided coal. 2. The method according to claim 1 wherein the finely divided coal is provided by crushing a coal stock. 3. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the stream of air has a flow rate of about 40 mL/min. 4. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein in step (b), the heating is carried out for a period of time between about 1 hour and about 10 hours. 5. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the coal is selected from the group consisting of lignite, sub-bituminous coal, bituminous coal, anthracite, coal tailings and any combination thereof. 6. The method according to claim 5, wherein the coal is lignite. 7. The method according to claim 6, further comprising a step before step (a), wherein the step before step (a) comprises drying the coal by heating at a temperature of between about 50Â°C and about 110Â°C. 8. The method according to claim 7, wherein the heating is carried out for a period of time between about 1 hour and about 24 hours. 9. The method according to any one of claims 6 to 8, wherein the finely divided coal has a particle size of between about 50 pm and about 5 mm. 10. The method according to any one of claims 6 to 9, wherein the heating at step (b) is carried out at a temperature of between about 150Â°C and about 250Â°C. 11. The method according to claim 5, wherein the coal is bituminous coal. 12. The method according to claim 11, wherein the finely divided coal has a particle size of between about 50 pm and about 500 pm. 13. The method according to claim 11 or claim 12, further comprising a step before step (b), wherein the step comprises pre-treating the finely divided coal with a catalyst. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein the catalyst is selected from the group consisting of cerium oxide, iron oxide, magnesium oxide, manganese oxide, molybdenum oxide, tungsten oxide, vanadium oxide, zinc oxide, titanium dioxide, cobalt-titanium oxide, nickel-titanium oxide, barium titanate and lanthanum cobaltite, and any combination thereof. 15. The method according to claim 14, wherein the catalyst is titanium dioxide. 16. The method according to any one of claims 11 to 15, wherein the heating at step (b) is carried out at a temperature of between about 250Â°C and about 400Â°C. 17. The method according to claim 5, wherein the coal is coal tailings. 18. The method according to claim 17 wherein the heating at step (b) is carried out at a temperature of between about 250Â°C and about 350Â°C. 19. An activated coal composition, produced by the method according to any one of claims 1 to 18. 20. The activated coal composition according to claim 19, wherein the coal is lignite, and wherein a total content of oxygen-containing functional groups is between about 3.50 mmol/g and about 4.00 mmol/g. 21. The activated coal composition according to claim 20, wherein the composition has an adsorption capacity of ammonia of between about 60 mg/g and about 90 mg/g. 22. The activated coal composition according to claim 19, wherein the coal is bituminous coal, and wherein a total content of oxygen-containing functional groups is 

between about 2.00 mmol/g and about 2.50 mmol/g. 23. The activated coal composition according to claim 22, wherein the composition has an adsorption capacity of ammonia of between about 30 mg/g and about 60 mg/g. 24. An activated coal composition, wherein a pH of the composition is less than about pH 6.5, when a mixture of coal and water is measured at a ratio of 1:10. 25. The activated coal composition according to claim 24, wherein the pH is less than about pH 5.0. 26. The activated coal composition according to claim 24 or claim 25, wherein the coal is comprised of lignite. 27. The activated coal composition according to claim 26, wherein a total content of oxygen-containing functional groups is between about 3.50 mmol/g and about 4.00 mmol/g. 28. The activated coal composition according to claim 26 or claim 27, wherein the composition has an adsorption capacity of ammonia of between about 60 mg/g and about 90 mg/g. 29. The activated coal composition according to claim 24, wherein the coal is comprised of bituminous coal. 30. The activated coal composition according to claim 29, and wherein a total content of oxygen-containing functional groups is between about 2.00 mmol/g and about 2.50 mmol/g. 31. The activated coal composition according to claim 29 or claim 30, wherein the composition has an adsorption capacity of ammonia of between about 30 mg/g and about 60 mg/g. 32. A method of lowering the amount of ammonia and/or ammonium ion produced by animal waste, comprising administering to an animal enclosure which comprises the animal waste an effective amount of an activated coal composition according to any one of claims 19 to 31. 33. Use of an activated coal composition according to any one of claims 19 to 31, for lowering the amount of ammonia and/or ammonium ion produced by animal waste. 34. A method of activating a coal composition, comprising: (a) providing a finely divided coal; and (b) heating the finely divided coal, under aerobic conditions, to provide the activated coal composition, wherein the activated coal composition has an adsorption capacity of ammonia of between about 30 mg/g and about 90 mg/g.
